hypothesis: If open-weight language models are first supervised-fine-tuned on known BCC/B2 compositions and then optimized via Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical preference feedback, then they will generate alloy pairs whose simulated phase behavior meets the target coexistence/sequence/room-temperature/impurity objectives more frequently and with higher validity, coverage, novelty, and reward than SFT-only and baseline methods, because physics-based preferences align the model’s generation with thermodynamic constraints while limiting distribution shift.
